body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) topped the net foreign buy list with 86.65 million shares, with a total trading value of IDR 1.46 trillion, and its stock closed at IDR 4,160, up IDR 70 from the previous day. Meanwhile, PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) recorded a net foreign buy of 63.13 million shares, with a total trading value of IDR 18.14 billion, and its shares closed at IDR 89, up IDR 3 from the previous day.
title: Bank Rakyat Indonesia tops net foreign buy list with 86.65 million shares.

body: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market sold a net 195.25 million shares on Thursday, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (GOTO) and PT Bukalapak.com TBK (BUKA) being the top two sold companies, with net sales of 220.63 million and 88.81 million shares, respectively. On the other hand, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK (BBRI) and PT Bank Mandiri TBK (BMRI) were the top two bought companies, with net purchases of 196.43 million and 142.04 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market sell 195.25 million shares, with PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia and PT Bank Mandiri among top buys.

body: Jeffrey Limas Lim acquired 5.32% of PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk's shares, purchasing 212.81 million shares, while DBS Bank Ltd's Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund increased its stake in PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk to 8.6% by buying 6.36 million shares. Meanwhile, HSBC-Fund Svs A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self sold the most shares, releasing 65.16 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Tbk, a coal mining holding firm.
title: Private investors and foreign entities make notable transactions in Indonesian stock market.

body: Indonesia's foreign debt increased by 5.38% to USD 424.06 billion in November 2024, with the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) being the largest creditor, holding USD 21.28 billion. The country's foreign debt structure consists of USD 44.80 billion from international organizations, including the Asian Development Bank (ADB) and International Monetary Fund (IMF), and USD 177.51 billion from other sources.
title: Indonesia's foreign debt increases by 5.38% to USD 424.06 billion in November 2024, with International Bank for Reconstruction and Development being the largest creditor.

body: MB Investment Management Pte Ltd and Inv Management Pte Ltd sold 300,000 and 7.26 million shares of CYBR, respectively, reducing their ownership stakes to 27.45% and 46.34%, respectively. Meanwhile, various investors, including Phillip Securities Pte Ltd, PT Sarana Kelola Investa, and DBS Bank Ltd (DBS), made significant purchases and sales of shares in companies such as PT Sumber Mas Konstruksi Tbk, PT Meratus Jasa Prima Tbk, and PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk, affecting their ownership percentages.
title: Multiple investors engage in significant share transactions in various Indonesian companies.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (IHSG) closed its first session at 7,142.30 points, slightly down from the opening, but remained in the green zone throughout the session, supported by strengthening large-cap stocks in the energy and banking sectors. Gainers in the energy sector included PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK, PT Pertamina Geothermal TBK, and PT Adaro Minerals Indonesia TBK, while banking stocks such as PT Bank Central Asia TBK, PT Bank Mandiri TBK, and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK also saw gains.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Closes Slightly Down but Remains in Green Zone

body: Indonesia's non-oil gas imports reached USD 197.34 billion in 2024, with China being the largest source, accounting for USD 71.62 billion (36.28% of total imports), a 15.18% increase from 2023. Japan ranked second, with imports valued at USD 14.86 billion, a 9.56% decline from 2023, while imports from both countries saw significant increases in December 2024 compared to the same period in 2023.
title: Indonesia's non-oil gas imports reach USD 197.34 billion in 2024, with China and Japan being the largest sources.

body: The Financial Services Authority (OJK) has authorized debt collection services for non-performing loans from online lending platforms operating through peer-to-peer lending, with guidelines outlining borrower notification and operator responsibility for collection processes. Violations of these regulations may result in criminal sanctions, including imprisonment of 2-10 years and fines ranging from IDR 25 billion to IDR 250 billion.
title: Indonesia's Financial Services Authority permits debt collection services for non-performing loans from online lending platforms with strict guidelines.

body: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk (PJKK) has announced that it has IDR 118.29 billion remaining from its initial public offering (IPO) funds, which are currently held in accounts at PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) with interest rates of 3-4% per annum. The company has so far used IDR 27.1 billion for capital expenditure and IDR 218.54 billion for a capital injection into its subsidiary, PT Tamtama Perkasa, with its shares increasing 2.57% to IDR 13,950 per share in today's trading.
title: Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Reports Remaining IPO Funds of IDR 118.29 Billion

body: PT Sunindo Pratama Tbk (SUNI) has won a tender from PT Pertamina Hulu Sanga Sanga to supply COO casing pipes worth IDR 129.89 billion, which is expected to contribute to the company's revenue and operating profit over the next two years. In September 2024, SUNI's sales surged 67.8% year-on-year to IDR 800.1 billion, with net profit skyrocketing nearly 130% to IDR 168.67 billion.
title: PT Sunindo Pratama wins tender from PT Pertamina Hulu Sanga Sanga to supply oil pipes worth IDR 129.89 billion.

body: PT Platinum Wahab Nusantara Tbk (TGU) raised IDR 112.17 billion from its initial public offering in July 2023, with IDR 44.86 billion allocated for working capital and IDR 67.30 billion for expansion and development of stores and food trucks. The remaining IDR 1.67 billion has been placed in a bank index deposit at an interest rate of 4.25% until December 2024, with a total of 3.57 billion shares listed and a shareholding structure comprising 70% controllers and 30% non-controllers.
title: Teguk Beverage Chain Raises IDR 110.44 Billion from Initial Public Offering

body: The curators of PT Sri Rejeki Isman Tbk (Sritex) have identified irregularities following the bankruptcy ruling, including unpaid creditor claims of IDR 32.6 trillion, with one claim from an affiliated company worth IDR 1.2 trillion. The curators have also found that Sritex group still has ample raw materials, contradicting reports of a raw material crisis, and are struggling to freeze Sritex's accounts and meet with the company's owners.
title: Curators uncover irregularities and contradictions in Sritex's bankruptcy process, including hidden raw materials and operating subsidiaries.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) surged 1.77% to 7,079.56 on Wednesday, driven by foreign investors' net buy of IDR 593.59 billion, with top contributors being PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA). The JCI's gain was supported by a uniform increase in sectoral indices, with only basic materials and industrials experiencing declines, and total trading value reaching IDR 10.76 trillion.
title: Jakarta Composite Index surges 1.77% to 7,079.56, driven by foreign investors and banking stocks.

body: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market recorded a net sell of 124.51 million shares on Wednesday, January 15, with PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top two most sold stocks, with net sells of 124.51 million and 121.45 million shares, respectively. Meanwhile, PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) was the biggest net buy, with foreign investors purchasing 116.59 million shares, followed by PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), which saw net buys of 27.49 million and 108.18 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market show mixed sentiment, with net sells in companies like PT Surya Citra Media and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia, but net buys in PT Smartfren Telecom and national banks.

body: Bank Indonesia cut its benchmark interest rate to 5.75% in a surprise move, aiming to boost domestic economic growth despite the rupiah's recent weakness against the US dollar. The decision marks a shift in focus towards growth, but also presents a dual challenge for the central bank, which must balance supporting the economy with addressing rupiah depreciation.
title: Bank Indonesia cuts benchmark interest rate to 5.75% in surprise move to boost economic growth.

body: PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk (BBHI) has fully utilized the IDR 4.8 trillion proceeds from its limited public offering, allocating 85% towards credit financing, 10% for IT infrastructure development, and 5% for operational development. The bank is projecting double-digit loan growth of at least 10% by 2025, driven by its commitment to delivering superior value to shareholders while maintaining prudent banking practices.
title: Allo Bank Fully Utilizes IDR 4.8 Trillion from Rights Issue for Credit Financing, IT Infrastructure, and Operational Development.

body: Indonesia's trade balance surplus for oil, gas, and non-oil gas commodities declined 15.84% to USD 31.04 billion in 2024, driven by a drop in non-oil gas trade surplus to USD 51.44 billion and a widening oil gas trade deficit to USD 20.39 billion. Total imports rose 5.30% to USD 233.65 billion, while total exports increased 2.29% to USD 264.70 billion, with a non-oil gas trade balance surplus of USD 3.99 billion in December 2024.
title: Indonesia's trade balance surplus declines 15.84% in 2024 due to reduction in non-oil gas trade surplus and increase in oil gas trade deficit.

body: PT Cashlez Worldwide Indonesia Tbk (CASH) will launch its Management Employee Stock Option Plan (MESOP) Phase I from January 22-31, 2025, with a total value of IDR 2.39 billion, comprising 35.77 million options at an exercise price of IDR 67 per share. As of December 2024, Cashlez's share price stood at IDR 72 per share, with a total of 1.42 billion shares and a shareholder structure consisting of 30.47% controlling shareholders and 69.53% non-controlling shareholders.
title: Cashlez to Launch Management Employee Stock Option Plan Phase I with 35.77 Million Options.

body: PT Jobubu Jarum Minahasa Tbk (no ticker provided) is changing its initial public offering (IPO) proceeds utilization plan, seeking shareholder approval to reallocate funds from land acquisition to factory lease and renovation, citing the need for a more effective and efficient location to sustain production volume. The company had realized IDR 152.35 billion in net IPO proceeds, initially allocated for working capital, production plant construction, and land acquisition, but now plans to focus on leasing a factory in Central Java and developing new products to be launched in 2025.
title: PT Jobubu Jarum Minahasa Tbk to Revise IPO Proceeds Utilization Plan from Land Acquisition to Factory Lease.

body: PT Puri Global Sukses Tbk (PURI) booked IDR 332 billion in revenue from apartment sales in Batam, with a total target revenue of IDR 1.5 trillion for the project, and is focusing on completing Phase 1 with a revenue target of IDR 514 billion in 2025. The company's shares have seen a significant increase, rising 1.38% to IDR 294 and 34.86% over the past month, following the news of its revenue growth.
title: PT Puri Global Sukses Tbk Records IDR 332 Billion Revenue from Apartment Sales in Batam.
